page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.i1.xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon E5-2682v4 processor built on the x86_64 architecture, delivering 4 dedicated physical cores running at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz. The CPU includes 33 MB of L3 cache and supports instruction sets such as AVX, AVX512F, AES, and FMA. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

This instance includes 223 GB of bundled local SSD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. In benchmark evaluations, the hardware exhibits entry-level performance characteristics. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests place the instance in the bottom 25% of benchmarked servers. Memory bandwidth performance is similarly constrained, with large-block read operations falling into the bottom 10% and write operations in the bottom 25%. Cryptographic and compression tasks, such as gzip compression and bzip3 decompression, also register in the lower tiers of performance.

Given its hardware profile, the ecs.i1.xlarge offers a qualitative balance of dedicated CPU resources and local SSD storage for budget-conscious deployments that do not require high-throughput processing. Database operations and static web serving throughput are modest, with Redis SET operations achieving 2.98 million operations per second. This instance is best suited for lightweight general-purpose workloads, basic file serving, development environments, and low-traffic applications where dedicated CPU cores are preferred over shared-core alternatives.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.i1.xlarge is an x86_64 virtual server featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon E5-2682v4 cores running at 2.5 GHz, paired with 16.0 GB of RAM and 223 GB of bundled local SSD storage. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, this instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates entry-level performance, with CPU and memory bandwidth results generally falling into the bottom 25% of tested systems. Database operations and static web serving throughput are also modest. Consequently, this server is best suited for lightweight general-purpose workloads, development environments, and low-traffic applications that benefit from dedicated CPU resources and local storage without requiring high-throughput computational or memory performance.
